dispositions.rationale: Circle acquired Poloniex and later spun it out, but the Poloniex exchange identity continued rather than becoming a distinct successor entity.
BitBay changed its operating brand through Zonda to zondacrypto, establishing direct one-to-one identity continuity.
CoinCorner acquired Coinfloor's customer base, but CoinCorner is not currently a canonical HEI entity and no thin successor record will be created only to fill lineage fields.
The record describes a transition from Bgogo to Gogoswap, but Gogoswap is not a canonical HEI entity and the available lineage remains insufficient for a forced link.
Bitholic was rebranded as Bithumb Singapore, but the regional successor is not a canonical HEI entity and should not be conflated with the general Bithumb record.
ANX acquired the Justcoin brand and domain and revived the same service; this is ownership history rather than a clean identity succession to ANXPRO.
zondacrypto is the current identity in the direct BitBay to Zonda to zondacrypto brand lineage.
ANX acquired CoinMKT while the CoinMKT brand continued under the group; acquisition does not establish a direct successor identity to ANXPRO.
CryptalDash is described as transitioning to DLTify, but DLTify is not currently a canonical HEI entity and continuity cannot be represented safely yet.
Atomic Trade is associated with a transition to Banx.io, but Banx.io is not canonical and the available source quality is not strong enough for a forced identity link.
BTCChina officially adopted the BTCC brand, creating direct one-to-one brand continuity suitable for reciprocal lineage fields.
LocalEthereum changed its name to LocalCryptos within the same service identity; the in-place rebrand is already represented inside one umbrella entity.
CHBTC transitioned to the ZB.com brand, establishing direct one-to-one exchange identity continuity.
Bitvavo acquired LiteBit's customer base and supported customer account or asset migration; the source-side endpoint is valid without forcing a singular reciprocal predecessor on Bitvavo.
Kraken acquired Crypto Facilities, but the company and regulated venue continued within the group; this is ownership history rather than replacement by the Kraken exchange identity.
Kraken acquired Bit Trade Australia and integrated the service into its Australian operation; retain a one-way source endpoint because Kraken has multiple acquired lineages.
Qryptos was folded into or rebranded under Liquid, but Liquid is not currently a canonical HEI entity and no thin target record should be added only for lineage completion.
BarterDEX evolved into AtomicDEX, but AtomicDEX is not currently a canonical HEI identity and the relationship remains documented without a forced link.
BayBit.io is reported as transitioning to Vero Exchange, but Vero Exchange is not canonical and the available sources are largely secondary.
Kraken acquired BCM Exchange and migrated or transitioned its users; preserve the source-side endpoint without forcing one of several acquisitions into Kraken's singular predecessor field.
AlfaCashier rebranded to Alfacash, but Alfacash is not currently represented as a separate canonical HEI entity.
Anyswap adopted the Multichain identity, but Multichain is not currently a canonical HEI entity and the successor should not be invented solely to fill the field.
Huobi Korea separated from the wider Huobi or HTX group through ownership restructuring; independence did not create a successor exchange identity.
The v3 to dYdX Chain transition was a protocol and token migration within the same umbrella dYdX identity, not a predecessor-successor split.
Trader Joe changed its public brand to LFJ while remaining the same protocol family already represented by one HEI entity.
